rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH MILLER ET AL VS. CRANE CO. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Substituting Successor-In-Interest For Deceased Plaintiff
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thurday, January 17, 2019 in Department 503, Line 1. Plaintiff's motion for order substituting successor-in-interest for deceased plaintiff is granted. No opposition. Pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 377.32, Regina Jo Miller is hereby substituted as successor-in-interest for deceased plaintiff, Joseph Miller. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30 a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)

rulings.Case Title: CORNELIO CASTILLO ET AL VS. CRANE CO.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder To Fluor Daniel Illinois, Inc. S Motion For An Order Transferring Venue Of This Action To San Diego County

Defendant Southern California Edison Company's joinder in Defendant Flour Daniel Illinois, Inc.'s motion for an order transferring venue of this action to San Diego County is off calendar per the request of moving party. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)

rulings.Case Title: STANLEY BANDY ET AL VS. CARRIER CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thurday, January 17, 2019 in Department 503, Line 3.
